title: I realized my problem wasn’t finding trades… it was not rejecting bad ones
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
I spent a lot of time trying to find “good trades”.

Indicators.  

Patterns.  

AI tools.

Didn’t really help.

Then I reviewed my losing trades.

Most of them had something in common:

They shouldn’t have been taken in the first place.

\- weak structure  

\- unclear entry  

\- no real invalidation  

Not bad ideas. Just not solid enough.

So I changed one thing:

Instead of searching for setups,  

I focused on rejecting them.

Now before entering, I ask:

\- is the level clear?  

\- is the entry clean?  

\- does the invalidation make sense?  

If one is off → I skip.

Fewer trades. Way better consistency.

Hard part is being objective every time.

So I built a small tool that analyzes a chart screenshot and gives a structured breakdown of the setup.

Helps me remove bias before entering.

Curious if others here are using AI more for filtering than predicting.

If you want to check it out:  

https://herculmarket.com/
